items.hook: Space Shuttle Columbia launches on STS-93, with Eileen Collins becoming the first female space shuttle commander. The shuttle also carried and deployed the Chandra X-ray Observatory.
items.body: Space Shuttle Columbia (OV-102) was a Space Shuttle orbiter manufactured by Rockwell International and operated by NASA. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Former River City actor Iain Robertson jailed for nine years for rape
items.body: The 45-year-old, played Stevie O'Hara in the BBC soap, was convicted of five offences against the women between 2004 and 2019.

items.hook: Air by Christian Kracht review – a metaphysical jigsaw puzzle
items.body:
A portal opens from our modern world of data centres and interior design into a medieval fantasy realm, in this playfully surreal tale from the author of Eurotrash

A reclusive interior designer named Paul travels from his home in O

items.hook: The Rayleigh bath chair murder occurs in Rayleigh, Essex, England.
items.body: The Rayleigh bath chair murder was a 1943 incident of patricide in which a disabled man was killed in Rayleigh, Essex, England. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: BFI reveals next director of Filmmaking Funds
items.body: New director will take over from Mia Bays in October.

items.hook: She and Allan is a novel by H.
items.body: Rider Haggard, first published in 1921. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: A Knights Hospitaller fleet scores a crushing victory over an Aydinid fleet off Chios.
items.body: The Order of Knights of the Hospital of Saint John of Jerusalem, commonly known as the Knights Hospitaller, is a Catholic military order. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Watch the total solar eclipse with ESA in León
items.body: Discover the science behind one of nature's most extraordinary phenomena and experience a total solar eclipse with the European Space Agency (ESA), the City of León and the University of León.

items.hook: The Canoe and the Saddle (1862) is a novelized adventure memoir by the American author Theodore Winthrop (1828–1861).
items.body: It vividly describes Washington state’s landscape and natural resources as well as the tumultuous relationship between Winthrop and the Native American people he interacted with. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Rebel Angels is the second book in a fantasy trilogy by Libba Bray.
items.body: It is the sequel to A Great and Terrible Beauty and continues the story of Gemma Doyle, a girl in the late 19th century with the power of second sight. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Three bombs explode in the Naama Bay area of Sharm El Sheikh, Egypt, killing 88 people.
items.body: The 2005 Sharm El Sheikh bombings were committed by Islamist group Abdullah Azzam Brigades on 23 July 2005 in the Egyptian resort city of Sharm El Sheikh, at the southern tip of the Sinai Peninsula. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Three hundred colonists bound for New France depart from Dieppe, France.
items.body: New France was the territory colonized by France in mainland North America, beginning with the exploration of the Gulf of Saint Lawrence by Jacques Cartier in 1534 and ending with the cession of New France to Great Britain and Spain in 1763 under the Treaty of Paris. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Callipygia: Further Adventures in Terra Magica is a fantasy novel by American writer Lin Carter, the fourth and last in his series about the fictional "Flat Earth" of Terra Magica.
items.body: It was first published in paperback by DAW Books in February 1988. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Province of Canada is created by the Act of Union.
items.body: The Province of Canada was a British colony in British North America from 1841 to 1867. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: What Can't Wait is a young adult novel by Ashley Hope Pérez, published by Carolrhoda Lab in 2011.
items.body: The story portrays a Mexican American teenage girl living in Houston who is torn between the demands of her family and her ambitions for the future. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The hotel and restaurant were refurbished in 2007.
items.body: Manuc's Inn is the oldest operating hotel building in Bucharest, Romania. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Invite review – A-list ensemble electrify hilarious couples night gone wrong comedy
items.body:
Sundance film festival: Olivia Wilde, Seth Rogen, Penelope Cruz and Edward Norton are exceptional in a smart and funny winner about sex, marriage and partner-swapping

items.hook: The Odyssey film fans inspired to go back to the source
items.body: Book sales and audiobook figures for Homer's original poem rise sharply after the film's release.

items.hook: Seinfeld actor apologises to reality star for 'inappropriate' sketch when she was 17
items.body: Jason Alexander said he "truly regrets" the Funny Or Die sketch he appeared in with Courtney Stodden.
